name: Chulilla and Loriguilla Reservoir Loop
name_original: El Embalse de Loriguilla — Ruta circular desde Chulilla
description: This difficult 15.9-mile (25.6 km) touring cycling route from Chulilla offers challenging climbs and stunning views of the Loriguilla Reserv
region_intro: Touring cycling around Chulilla offers routes through dramatic landscapes shaped by the Turia River Canyon, featuring vertical walls and the Turia River. The region is characterized by the Serrania mountains, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Overlooking the village, the Chulilla Castle sits atop a hill, offering panoramic views. The diverse ecosystem includes pines, holm oaks, and other native flora. Best touring cycling routes around Chulilla The most popular touring cycling route is Chulilla and Loriguilla Reservoir Loop, a 15.9 miles (25.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 7 minutes to complete. This challenging route features significant elevation gain and views of the Loriguilla Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of Chulilla and the castle – Chulilla Viewpoint loop from Chulilla, a difficult 25.2 miles (40.6 km) path. This route offers panoramic vistas of the town and surrounding Serrania mountains. Local touring cyclists also love the Chulilla – Charco Azul Viewpoint loop from Losa del Obispo, a 12.7 miles (20.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views towards the Turia River Canyon and Charco Azul, often completed in about 1 hour 17 minutes. Touring cycling around Chulilla is defined by the Turia River Canyon, the Hanging Bridges, and the Chulilla Castle. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Chulilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Chulilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Chulilla?
A: Chulilla offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 50 options available. While many are challenging, there are also moderate routes to explore the dramatic landscapes of the Turia River Canyon and the Serrania mountains.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Chulilla?
A: The majority of touring cycling routes in Chulilla are considered difficult, with 48 out of 55 routes falling into this category. However, there are also 7 moderate routes available, offering options for different fitness levels. Expect varied terrain, including significant elevation changes.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see while cycling in Chulilla?
A: While touring cycling around Chulilla, you can experience the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/829646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turia River Suspension Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and the stunning Turia River Canyon. Other notable natural features include the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3028573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco Azul of Chulilla&lt;/a&gt;, a natural pool, and various caves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5359267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de la Garita&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features historical landmarks such as the Peña Cortada Aqueduct.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Chulilla?
A: Yes, Chulilla offers several circular touring cycling routes. For instance, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e918662334/chulilla-and-loriguilla-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chulilla and Loriguilla Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; provides stunning views of the reservoir. Another option is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Chulilla and the castle – Chulilla Viewpoint loop from Chulilla&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic vistas of the town and surrounding mountains.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: While most routes in Chulilla are challenging, there are moderate options available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38164567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chulilla – Charco Azul Viewpoint loop from Losa del Obispo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 20.4 km trail that offers views towards the Turia River Canyon and Charco Azul, making it a good choice for those seeking a less strenuous ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Chulilla?
A: Chulilla is an excellent destination for outdoor activities, including touring cycling, for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists in Chulilla?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Chulilla village for visitors. Specific parking areas for accessing trailheads may vary, but the village itself provides options for general visitor parking, from which many routes are accessible.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Chulilla?
A: While Chulilla's natural environment is welcoming, specific regulations for dogs on cycling routes can vary. It's advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected natural areas or near wildlife. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them to preserve the natural beauty of the area.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Chulilla?
A: Given that most touring cycling routes in Chulilla are rated as difficult, options specifically designed as 'family-friendly' for younger children or less experienced riders are limited. The moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38164567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chulilla – Charco Azul Viewpoint loop from Losa del Obispo&lt;/a&gt;, might be suitable for families with older, more experienced cycling members.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay for cyclists in Chulilla?
A: Chulilla is a charming village with narrow, winding streets and whitewashed houses, offering various local cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. Cyclists can find places to refuel and rest after their rides within the village, enjoying local cuisine and hospitality.

Q: Is it possible to reach Chulilla with public transport and a bike?
A: Public transport options to Chulilla are available, primarily by bus from Valencia. However, policies regarding transporting bicycles on public buses can vary. It's recommended to check with the specific bus operator in advance to confirm their bike carriage rules and any associated fees or restrictions.

Q: What do other cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Chulilla?
A: The touring cycling experience in Chulilla is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 6 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the dramatic landscapes, the challenging terrain, and the stunning views of the Turia River Canyon and the Loriguilla Reservoir.

Q: Which touring cycling routes offer views of the Loriguilla Reservoir?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e918662334/chulilla-and-loriguilla-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chulilla and Loriguilla Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 25.6 km route specifically designed to provide stunning views of the Loriguilla Reservoir, making it a popular choice for those seeking scenic water vistas.
tips: From here you will have magnificent views of the beautiful town of Chulilla and the cliffs of the Turia River where the famous Charco Azul is located.

A very picturesque and quiet place. The views are very nice and can be reached by road in good condition. There is parking and from here you can start several routes that can end in Chulilla or Sot de Chera.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'difficult' for touring cyclists. It covers 25.6 km with a significant elevation gain of over 600 meters. While mostly paved, some segments may be unpaved, and you might need very good fitness to complete it comfortably. Be prepared for uphill sections that can be challenging.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features paved surfaces, making it suitable for touring bikes. However, be aware that some sections may involve unpaved dirt or gravel roads, often referred to as 'forest highways'. For example, parts of the PR-CV 77 Chulilla-Embalse de Loriguilla are known to be dirt/gravel.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience breathtaking views of the Turia River canyon with its dramatic cliffs. Key highlights include the picturesque Loriguilla Reservoir and the tranquil Charco Azul. Don't miss the panoramic vistas from the Chulilla Viewpoint.

faqs.question: Does the route pass by the famous Chulilla hanging bridges?
faqs.answer: While the iconic hanging bridges of Chulilla are a major attraction in the area, they are primarily associated with hiking routes like the Ruta de los Pantaneros. This touring bicycle route may pass near them, but they are generally not directly traversable on a bike and would require dismounting or taking an alternative path.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Chulilla. You can find parking options within the village, which serves as a convenient base for exploring the area. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked for visitors.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for cycling this route, with milder temperatures and beautiful natural scenery. Summers can be very hot, especially in the Turia River canyon, while winters might bring cooler temperatures and potentially more unpredictable weather.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dogs specifically for this cycling route is not detailed. However, when cycling in natural areas, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially if sharing paths with hikers or in areas with wildlife. Always ensure your dog is fit enough for the distance and terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: This route is located within the Paraje Natural Municipal Los Calderones. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public trails within this natural park. However, always respect local regulations and signage regarding access and conservation.

faqs.question: Are there any sections where I might need to dismount or push my bike?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' classification and significant elevation gain, it's possible that some steep or unpaved uphill sections might require you to dismount and push your touring bicycle, especially if you're not accustomed to challenging climbs or if the terrain is particularly loose.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it includes sections of the PR-CV 77 Chulilla-Embalse de Loriguilla, which makes up about 10% of the route. You'll also encounter parts of the Camino de Cubillas PRCV 414 and the Ruta de los Pantaneros.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Chulilla, where you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel before or after your ride. Along the route itself, especially in the more natural sections, services will be limited, so it's best to carry sufficient water and snacks.
